notes: Server 3.40 adds an opt-in Compatibility rule for 10-bit HEVC. Root cause of the Euphoria S2/S3 playback problem: those files are 10-bit HEVC (Main 10) SDR, and some TVs have an 8-bit-only HEVC decoder that can't direct-play 10-bit — the file black-screens unless the client bitrate cap forces a transcode down to 8-bit. It was never bitrate or subtitles. New setting 'Flag 10-bit HEVC (8-bit-only TVs)' (Settings → Compatibility, default OFF) makes the Compat scan flag 10-bit HEVC SDR files and re-encode them to 8-bit so they direct-play; HDR/Dolby Vision is never touched (the DV pipeline owns that). Left OFF by default because most modern devices decode 10-bit HEVC fine and re-encoding is lossy. Node stays 2.33 (mkvmerge exit-1 fix + scaler fix) — update both nodes to 2.33 if not already.
